author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
But Why Should One Be Punished More Than Another?
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: But Why Should One Be Punished More Than Another?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “But if,” it is said, “it was necessary that, although all were not condemned, He should still show what was due to all, and so He should commend His grace more freely to the vessels of mercy; why in the same case will He punish me more than another, or deliver him more than me?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: If you ask wherefore; because I confess that I can find no answer to make.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And if you further ask why is this, it is because in this matter, even as His anger is righteous and as His mercy is great, so His judgments are unsearchable.
